name: STARD
full_name: Standards for Reporting of Diagnostic Accuracy Studies

items.description: Identification as a study of diagnostic accuracy using at least one measure of accuracy
items.notes: Title should clearly identify the study as evaluating diagnostic accuracy

items.description: Structured abstract with study design, methods, results, and conclusions
items.notes: Abstract should include key accuracy metrics

items.description: Scientific and clinical background, including the intended use and clinical role of the index test
items.notes: Should clarify whether the test is for triage, replacement, or add-on

items.description: Study objectives and hypotheses
items.notes: Clear statement of study aims

items.description: Whether data collection was planned before the index test and reference standard were performed (prospective study) or after (retrospective study)
items.notes: Prospective vs retrospective design affects bias risk

items.description: Eligibility criteria
items.notes: Clear eligibility criteria define the study population

items.description: On what basis potentially eligible participants were identified
items.notes: Consecutive or random enrollment reduces spectrum bias

items.description: Where and when potentially eligible participants were identified (setting, location and dates)
items.notes: Setting and dates provide context for generalizability

items.description: Index test, in sufficient detail to allow replication
items.notes: Sufficient detail for test replication is essential

items.description: Reference standard, in sufficient detail to allow replication
items.notes: The reference standard must be clearly described and appropriate

items.description: Rationale for choosing the reference standard (if alternatives exist)
items.notes: Justification of the reference standard choice

items.description: Definition of and rationale for test positivity cut-offs or result categories of the index test, distinguishing pre-specified from exploratory
items.notes: Pre-specified vs exploratory thresholds affect validity

items.description: Definition of and rationale for test positivity cut-offs or result categories of the reference standard, distinguishing pre-specified from exploratory
items.notes: Clear case definition for the target condition

items.description: Whether clinical information and reference standard results were available to the performers/readers of the index test
items.notes: Blinding of index test interpretation prevents review bias

items.description: Whether clinical information and index test results were available to the assessors of the reference standard
items.notes: Blinding of reference standard interpretation prevents bias

items.description: Methods for estimating or comparing measures of diagnostic accuracy
items.notes: Statistical methods for accuracy estimation

items.description: How indeterminate index test or reference standard results were handled
items.notes: Handling of indeterminate results affects accuracy estimates

items.description: How missing data on the index test and reference standard were handled
items.notes: Missing data handling strategy

items.description: Any analyses of variability in diagnostic accuracy, distinguishing pre-specified from exploratory
items.notes: Subgroup analyses and sources of variation

items.description: Intended sample size and how it was determined
items.notes: Sample size justification for diagnostic accuracy studies

items.description: Flow of participants, using a diagram
items.notes: STARD flow diagram showing participant flow through the study

items.description: Baseline demographic and clinical characteristics of participants
items.notes: Demographics define the spectrum of tested patients

items.description: Distribution of severity of disease in those with the target condition
items.notes: Disease spectrum affects diagnostic accuracy (spectrum bias)

items.description: Cross tabulation of the index test results (or their distribution) by the results of the reference standard
items.notes: Core data table for calculating accuracy metrics

items.description: Estimates of diagnostic accuracy and their precision (such as 95% confidence intervals)
items.notes: Primary outcome measures with confidence intervals

items.description: Any adverse events from performing the index test or the reference standard
items.notes: Test-related harms should be reported

items.description: Study limitations, including sources of potential bias, statistical uncertainty, and generalizability
items.notes: Key diagnostic accuracy biases: spectrum, verification, incorporation, review bias

items.description: Implications for practice, including the intended use and clinical role of the index test
items.notes: How findings might be applied in clinical practice

items.description: Registration number and name of registry
items.notes: Study registration reduces selective reporting

items.description: Where the full study protocol can be accessed
items.notes: Protocol availability enables assessment of selective reporting

items.description: Sources of funding and other support; role of funders
items.notes: Funding sources and their role in the study
